todaysEvents.summary: Trump orders US naval blockade of Hormuz after Vance-Iran talks collapse without deal after 21 hours; Brent spikes +7% to ~$102 (est.); tail risk surges to 48% (Apr 13).
Iran: 'The Strait of Hormuz will not be opened'; IRGC warns of 'regretful response' to US warships; FM blames 'excessive US demands' on nuclear commitment and Hormuz sovereignty (Apr 13).
todaysEvents.delta: Iran defiant; direct US-Iran naval standoff imminent

todaysEvents.summary: Netanyahu signals Israel-Lebanon negotiations begin Tuesday Apr 14, mediated by US; Pakistan FM urges both sides to maintain ceasefire; talks could resume (Apr 13).
todaysEvents.delta: Lebanon de-escalation signal offsets blockade risk

scenarios.label: Base
scenarios.description: US blockade serves as coercive leverage; Iran agrees to nuclear talks framework within 72 hours to avoid direct naval confrontation. Ceasefire preserved; Hormuz formally reopened under US escort arrangement. P&I restoration follows within 2-3 weeks. Brent retraces toward $90-95/bbl as risk premium unwinds; iTraxx Asia IG tightens below 170bp. Netanyahu-Lebanon deal on April 14 removes the core Iranian Hormuz pretext.

scenarios.label: Stress
scenarios.description: US blockade and Iran standoff persist for 1-2 weeks without direct kinetic engagement. Ceasefire nominally intact but effectively void - commercial traffic remains blocked. Further talks are attempted but no framework emerges on nuclear or Hormuz sovereignty. Brent stabilises $100-120/bbl; JKM $20-25/MMBtu; iTraxx Asia IG 190-220bp. IRGC conducts asymmetric harassment of US vessels without triggering full escalation.

scenarios.label: Tail
scenarios.description: US Navy enforces blockade; IRGC executes 'regretful response' - attacks US warships or commercial vessels transiting under US escort. Full ceasefire collapse; resumed US-Israel air campaign against Iranian infrastructure. Kharg Island seizure or Saudi Aramco attack brings GCC production under acute threat. Brent spikes $140-185/bbl; LNG markets seize; ASEAN recession risk becomes acute. Gold surges past $5,200/oz.
